paper_title: Corrosion of carbonate speleothems by an allogenic river inferred from petrography and a weight loss experiment: a case study from the Demänová Cave System, Slovakia

methods.description: Examination of speleothems and experimental tablets for corrosion traces and siliciclastic material using optical and scanning electron microscopes.

steps.description: Examine speleothems and experimental tablets using a standard optical microscope with thin sections.
steps.searchable_summary: use optical microscope to examine thin sections for corrosion traces

steps.description: Harden samples with epoxy resin before preparation.
Investigate surfaces using a scanning electron microscope coupled with an energy dispersive spectrometer (SEM-EDS).

steps.description: Coat specimens with carbon before SEM examination.

methods.description: Determination of mineral composition using X-ray diffraction (XRD) and electron probe microanalysis (EMPA).

steps.description: Analyze element distribution using electron probe microanalysis (EMPA) on polished thin sections.

methods.description: Dating of flowstone samples using U-series dating method, including sample preparation and isotopic composition measurement.

steps.description: Treat samples at high temperatures to decompose organic matter.

steps.description: Dissolve samples in nitric acid and separate uranium and thorium using chromatographic method.
